weather_report:
West Perth settles into a quiet late evening beneath a worn quilt of cloud, the sky breathing in broken patches with the moon occasionally slipping through. The night carries a muted glow and an easy pace; UV exposure is essentially nil now, with no sun until morning. Toward the early hours those clouds thin to scattered tufts, promising a clearer first light and a gentler, more open sky at dawn. Expect a day that begins soft rather than blazing, with sunshine filtered at first before it gathers itself.

It’s the kind of night for light layers and an easy jacket, something you can shrug off when the morning brightens. If you’re awake, brew a calming tea, sort tomorrow’s bag, or finish that lingering chapter; if you rise with the magpies, a brief walk to greet the early blue would suit the mood. Sunglasses and a dab of sunscreen will be wise once the day wakes, but for now the world is quiet, clear enough to hear your own thoughts.
